error.signature: AI tells a tourist they can open a UAE bank account easily on a visit visa

verdict.summary: Opening a UAE bank account generally requires a residence visa and an Emirates ID; tourists on a visit visa usually cannot open a standard current account (some banks offer limited non-resident savings accounts with high minimum balances). A salary certificate or proof of address is typically needed too.

dead_ends.action: Try to open a normal UAE current account while on a tourist or visit visa
dead_ends.why_fails: Standard current accounts require a residence visa and Emirates ID; visit-visa holders are typically declined, though some banks offer restricted non-resident savings accounts.
dead_ends.common_misconception: Dubai's openness to tourists does not extend to easy account opening.

dead_ends.action: Assume a non-resident account works like a local current account
dead_ends.why_fails: Non-resident accounts often have high minimum balances, no chequebook, and limited features - not suitable for receiving a local salary or daily use.
dead_ends.condition: Using a non-resident account for salary/daily banking

workarounds.action: Open the account after your residence visa and Emirates ID are issued, bringing passport, visa, Emirates ID, and a salary certificate or tenancy contract.
workarounds.how: Once your sponsor processes your residence visa and Emirates ID, visit a bank with those plus a salary certificate or proof of address.

workarounds.action: For short stays, use no-foreign-fee cards and ATMs rather than opening an account.
workarounds.how: Use international cards; the UAE is highly card-friendly in cities.
workarounds.tradeoff: ATM/FX fees apply; not for receiving local income.
